signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Options Skew + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.09 — 275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite the U.S. revoking Iranian oil waivers following Strait of Hormuz tanker attacks. JPY COT LF net short -137,828 (25.3% long) remains crowded, but 25d RR at -1.52 (strong put skew) signals the options market is aggressively paying for JPY protection against intervention or escalation. Carry dominates while VIX remains at 16.1, but intervention risk is acute above 162.00.
signals.watch_level: Watch for BoJ verbal intervention or a VIX spike above 25 to trigger carry unwinds.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -25,757 (19.5% long) shows speculators heavily fading the RBNZ despite a consensus-expected rate hike at the July 8 meeting. With NZD/USD at 0.5678 and CESI at +6 (positive data beats), a hawkish delivery or hike tomorrow creates a high-conviction short-squeeze risk as crowded spec shorts are forced to unwind. NZD real rate at -0.83% remains accommodative, supporting the RBNZ's hiking bias.
signals.watch_level: RBNZ rate decision on July 8 is the primary catalyst for a short-squeeze.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Positioning + Vol
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4197 — the 252d correlation with WTI Oil has broken to -0.881 (z=-2.04σ), as the pair remains bid despite WTI climbing +5.31% to $72.2 on supply disruptions. CAD COT LF net short -92,861 (20.6% long) reflects extreme institutional bearishness, while retail sentiment is 84% short (diverging from COT). This institutional vs retail divergence at the 1.42 level suggests a late-trend exhaustion; however, the 150bp Fed-BoC carry advantage continues to anchor the USD bid.
signals.watch_level: Watch for a break of 1.4200 or the BoC meeting on July 15 to shift the regime.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + Carry
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6928 — IV at 27.8% vs HV30 at 6.9% creates a massive +20.9% Vol Risk Premium, signaling the options market is pricing in extreme tail-risk protection relative to recent realized movement. Despite the high vol premium, the 60bp AUD carry advantage (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%) and positive CESI (+8) support the pair on dips. COT LF net long +21,597 confirms institutional support for the carry trade.
signals.watch_level: Watch for IV compression or RBA meeting on Aug 11 to validate the carry bid.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Data Underperformance + Hedging Demand
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1410 — persistent data misses (CESI -10) and a hawkish Fed's Williams support the 150bp USD carry advantage. EUR COT LF net short -83,016 (31.6% long) aligns with the negative data trend. EUR/USD IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.8% (+2.3% VRP) indicates elevated hedging demand as the pair tests the 1.1400 handle. Upside remains structurally limited by the real rate differential (USD +1.49% vs EUR +0.03%).
signals.watch_level: Watch for the ECB meeting on July 23 to address the widening data divergence.
